name: Google News: Corruption Dark Money
notes: Google News topic RSS — corruption, dark money, money laundering. Layer 2 topic-filtered. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Google News: Sanctions Evasion
notes: Google News topic RSS — sanctions evasion, shell companies, oligarch assets. Cui Bono intelligence. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Google News: Energy Security
notes: Google News topic RSS — energy security, oil price, gas supply. Physical reality intelligence. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Google News: Financial Stability
notes: Google News topic RSS — sanctions, financial stability, debt crisis. Cui Bono financial intelligence. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Yahoo News RSS
notes: Yahoo News general RSS — 50 articles of broad world news. Layer 4 wide-angle backup. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: EFF Digital Rights
notes: Electronic Frontier Foundation — digital rights, AI surveillance, privacy, cyber law. TIER1: original reporting and legal analysis. Best source for rights-contracting tech deployments. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Wired Magazine
notes: Wired — geopolitical tech coverage: AI chips, semiconductor wars, surveillance, cyber warfare, export controls. TIER2: analytical depth. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Ars Technica
notes: Ars Technica — deep tech analysis, AI, security, infrastructure. TIER2: specialist depth. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: IEEE Spectrum
notes: IEEE Spectrum — semiconductor engineering, AI hardware, critical infrastructure tech. TIER2: engineering specialist. Only source for semiconductor/chip-level intelligence. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Google News: IMF World Bank Economy
notes: Google News topic RSS — IMF, World Bank, debt restructuring, sovereign debt. Debt Trap intelligence. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Google News: Critical Minerals
notes: Google News topic RSS — critical minerals, rare earth, food security, water scarcity. Physical reality intelligence. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Google News: IEA Energy Transition
notes: Google News topic RSS — IEA, energy transition, renewable energy, energy poverty. GCSP physical foundation intelligence. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Financial Times World
notes: Financial Times — gold standard for financial intelligence. Primary source for sanctions, central bank signals, sovereign debt. TIER1: original reporting with institutional credibility. Reserve for BBC when BBC fails.

name: OCCRP — Organized Crime and Corruption
notes: Organized Crime and Corruption Reporting Project. Investigative journalism exposing money laundering networks, oligarch assets, state-crime nexus. TIER1: 430 official investigations triggered. Reserve for ICIJ when ICIJ fails.

name: Relief Web
notes: UN OCHA-managed humanitarian intelligence platform. Covers conflicts, disasters, displacement, humanitarian crises. TIER2: authoritative on human impact of military conflicts — fills ACLED gap.

name: Crisis Group
notes: International Crisis Group — conflict prevention gold standard. Read by UN Security Council, foreign ministries, peacekeepers. TIER2: deep conflict analysis not wire news. Was in GNI but died — now working again.

name: Google News: IMF
notes: Google News IMF topic RSS — replaces broken imf.org direct feed. Covers IMF statements, programs, country reviews. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Google News: World Bank
notes: Google News World Bank topic RSS — replaces broken blogs.worldbank.org direct feed. Covers WB lending, development finance, country programs. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Google News: Central Banks
notes: Google News topic RSS — central bank decisions, Federal Reserve, interest rates, inflation signals. Monetary policy intelligence layer. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Military Times
notes: Military Times — independent defense journalism covering Pentagon, Congress, all service branches. Replaces SIPRI 404. TIER2: daily reporting on procurement, policy, operations. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Breaking Defense
notes: Breaking Defense — deep defense policy analysis, Pentagon, NATO, Indo-Pacific strategy. TIER2: read by defense officials and analysts. Strongest source for early military posture signals.

name: CGTN — China Global Television Network
notes: Chinese state international broadcaster. Required for S2-D Adversary Narrative. Official Chinese narrative to global audiences. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: TASS — Russian News Agency
notes: Russian state news agency. Required for S2-D Adversary Narrative. Primary Kremlin narrative amplifier. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Global Times
notes: CCP hawkish nationalist outlet. Required for S2-D. More aggressive than Xinhua — used to test public reaction and signal redlines. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Press TV — Iran State Media
notes: Iranian state English broadcaster. Required for S2-D. Key adversarial narrative source for Middle East and anti-Western framing. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: TRT World — Turkey State Media
notes: Turkish state international broadcaster. NATO member with independent strategic narrative. Key for S2-D — bridges Western and adversarial framing. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Chatham House
notes: Royal Institute of International Affairs — UK gold standard foreign policy analysis. Read by UK Cabinet, FCO, NATO. TIER1: primary source for European strategic perspective. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Carnegie Endowment for International Peace
notes: Carnegie Endowment — nuclear policy, democracy erosion, US-China-Russia triangulation. TIER1: read by State Dept, NSC. Strongest source for long-cycle strategic shifts. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Brookings Institution
notes: Brookings — center-left US policy analysis. Economic policy, governance, democracy. TIER2: strong on domestic-to-foreign policy linkage. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: IISS — International Institute for Strategic Studies
notes: IISS — Military Balance authors. Gold standard for global military capability assessments. TIER1: used by every major defense ministry. Best source for hard military capability signals. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Access Now — Digital Rights
notes: Access Now — internet shutdowns, surveillance tech exports, authoritarian tech deployments. TIER1: documents real-world rights contractions with evidence. Complements EFF for global non-US cases. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Freedom House
notes: Freedom House — democracy erosion, authoritarian expansion, press freedom. TIER2: cited in UN and US Congress. Key for S2-E Legitimacy Filter. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Global Financial Integrity
notes: GFI — illicit financial flows, trade misinvoicing, dark money corridors. TIER2: quantitative analysis of hidden capital flows. Unique intelligence not covered by ICIJ/OCCRP. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: South China Morning Post
notes: SCMP — Hong Kong-based. Covers China with Western-style journalism standards. Key for reading Beijing signals that Xinhua/CGTN soft-pedal. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Google News: Influence Operations Disinformation
notes: Google News topic RSS — influence operations, disinformation campaigns, information warfare. Direct support for S2-A Injection Tracer and S2-B Coordination Analyzer. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Google News: Cognitive Warfare Propaganda
notes: Google News topic RSS — propaganda, cognitive warfare, psychological operations. Direct System 2 support for injection detection and adversary narrative tracking. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Google News: Geopolitical Competition Indo-Pacific
notes: Google News topic RSS — Indo-Pacific competition, South China Sea, Taiwan Strait, ASEAN. Fills significant geographic gap in current source coverage. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Google News: Election Interference Democratic Backsliding
notes: Google News topic RSS — election interference, democratic backsliding, authoritarian expansion. Direct support for S2-E Legitimacy Filter. LR-014(E) compliant.

name: Dawn (Pakistan)
note: Pakistan perspective on US-China-India triangle, South Asia, Iran. Non-Western analytical tradition.

name: Asia Times
note: Hong Kong-based. Indo-Pacific from Asian vantage. Strong on China, ASEAN, US-China decoupling.

name: Middle East Eye
note: MENA from regional perspective. Gulf, Palestine, Iran, Turkey. Diverges from Western MSM framing.

name: The Hindu
note: Indian strategic autonomy perspective. Refuses US-China binary. Critical for Global South signals.

name: Al Jazeera English
note: Qatar-funded. Global South and Muslim-majority world framing. Covers stories Western MSM ignores.

name: CGTN (China Global Television Network)
note: Chinese state international broadcaster. Reveals Beijing's desired global narrative. What China wants the world to believe = strategic signal.

name: Global Times
note: CCP-aligned English outlet. Nationalistic framing. Reveals domestic Chinese political pressures projected internationally.

name: Press TV
note: Iranian state international broadcaster. Reveals Tehran's desired global framing. Anti-Western, pro-resistance axis narrative.

name: RT (Russia Today) English
note: Russian state international broadcaster. Reveals Moscow's narrative strategy. Anti-NATO, multipolar world framing.

name: Al-Mayadeen
note: Lebanon-based, Iran/Hezbollah-aligned. Resistance axis perspective. Covers Gaza, Lebanon, Yemen from inside the axis.

name: TeleSUR English
note: Venezuelan state-backed Latin American broadcaster. Anti-US imperialism framing. Covers Global South from socialist perspective.

name: Valdai Discussion Club
notes: Elite Russian strategic think-tank. Editorial positions preview Russian foreign policy framing before it appears in state media. Divergence from Western framing on same events IS the intelligence signal.

name: Observer Research Foundation (ORF India)
notes: India's leading strategic think-tank. Non-Western, non-Chinese perspective — third pole analytical tradition. Indo-Pacific, multipolarity, South-South framing of same events as Western sources. India's strategic autonomy doctrine produces distinctly different geopolitical readings.

name: Institute for Security Studies (ISS Africa)
notes: Premier African security think-tank. African analytical tradition on governance, conflict, and state fragility differs substantially from Western framing. Reads the same African and global events through the lens of colonialism's structural legacy — which Western analysis systematically misses.

name: ISEAS Yusof Ishak Institute (ASEAN Studies)
notes: Singapore-based. Southeast Asian strategic perspective on US-China competition, ASEAN centrality, and regional order. ASEAN framing explicitly avoids forced binary alignment — the most important analytical tradition for understanding fence-sitter geopolitics that both US and Chinese narratives systematically distort.

name: Carnegie Endowment for International Peace
notes: Has offices in Moscow, Beijing, Beirut, Brussels, New Delhi, Berlin. The non-Washington Carnegie offices produce analysis from genuinely local perspectives — different from Western MSM. Carnegie Moscow and Carnegie China are especially valuable for non-adversarial expert framing of Russian and Chinese strategic thinking.

name: CASS Institute of World Economics and Politics
notes: Chinese Academy of Social Sciences international arm. Elite Chinese economic and strategic think-tank. Previews PBSC economic framing before state media. Divergence from Western economic analysis IS the signal.
